title: practical english usage - Part 103
content:
I admire your belief that you are always right.

Does she understand the need to keep everything secret?

I hate the thought of leaving you.

Luke's criticism of the plan made him  very unpopular.

can be prepositional phrases, infinitive expressions or clauses (with or without other words and expressions that ‘ complete' their meaning. These complements Many nouns, especially abstract nouns, can be followed by ‘ complements' - a  noun?

122 noun    + complement:        What    can   follow Nouns     and   Noun    Phrases;    Agreement        Section  11 grammar •  124  nouns in combination: my father's house I don 't like Alice's friends much.   Dan's eyes are like yours.

Emily's brother is a lawyer, (     The-brotker^fEmify-...) not That's my father's house, (     ... the-house-of my-father) animal, or to a country, organisation or other group of living creatures.

physical characteristics, especially when the first noun refers to a person or We   use the 's structure most often to talk about possessions, relationships and 2 meanings       of  the   's  structure stupid-boyfriend-. ..) not / saw that stupid boyfriend of Angie's yesterday, (     . .. that Angie's not She's a cousin of Jack's, (     . .. a-Jack's-ceusim) we  usually use the of mine, etc construction (►   177).

When we  want to use a noun with a/an or this/that, etc as well as a possessive, the car that is the boss's = the boss's car But a possessive word may of course have its own article.

not                               or the car that is Jack's = Jack's car (     the Jack's car   Jack's the car) possessives are used.

possessive word (►   143.3). Definite articles are usually dropped when A  noun cannot normally have an article or other determiner with it as well as a a car this car his car Jack's car Compare: A  possessive 's structure acts as a determiner (►  Section 12) in a noun phrase.

1  possessive        's  and   other     determiners 124 n ouns    in  co mbination:       my    father's       house Socrates' /'snkrətiɪzɪz/ ideas.

often pronounce a possessive 's even when it is not written.

But with singular classical (ancient Greek and Roman) names ending in s', we The  apostrophe in a word like parents' does not change the pronunciation at all.

president's /'prezɪdənts/ dog's /dogz/                           Alice's /'ælɪsiz/       James's doctor's /'doktəz/                 Jack's /dʒæks/            Madge's /`mæʤvzj The  ending 's is pronounced just like a plural ending (►   118).

Joe's and Anna's children (two separate lots of children: Joe's and Anna's) Joe and Anna's children (one lot of children: Joe and Anna are their parents) Note the difference between, for example: the Smiths' new house the man next door's wife Henry the Eighth's six wives We   can add 's or ' to a whole phrase.

But  's is more common.

Socrates' ideas Dickens' novels especially in literary and classical references.

We   sometimes just add an apostrophe (’ ) to a singular noun ending in -s, grammar •  124   nouns in combination: my father's house a three-hour journey   a twenty-minute delay (►   125.3).

Noun + noun structures are also possible in expressions with numbers a day's journey twenty minutes' delay The  's structure (or the plural with sɔ  is often used to say how  long things last.

5 measurement          of  time:   a  day's     journey the prisoner's release (they released the prisoner) In a few cases, the first noun may correspond to the object of a verb.

Harris's novel (Harris wrote a novel) the manager's decision (the manager made a decision) America's gold reserves (America has gold reserves) Joe's brother (Joe has a brother)   the dog's tail (the dog has a tail) is the subject of have or some other verb.

Note that the’ s structure often corresponds to a sentence in which  the first noun 4 like   a  subject choice of structure often depends on the particular expression.

Unfortunately, it is not possible to give useful general rules in this area: the not (     ... of the world), ►    206.7 or the world's oldest mountains   the oldest mountains in the world or the train's arrival   the arrival of the train or the concerto's final movement   the final movement of the concerto or the plan's importance  the importance of the plan or the earth's gravity   the gravity of the earth However, both structures are possible in some expressions.
